meta.attribution: Dataset curated by Chisom Rutherford. Source: chisomrutherford/nigeria-clinical-guidelines-dataset (HuggingFace)
meta.description: Nigeria-specific clinical guardrails derived from NSTG 2022 for use by the Adaptive Therapy Controller's safety layer

dose_limits.rationale: Cardiotoxicity threshold — irreversible cardiomyopathy above this dose

HIV_coinfection.prevalence_context: Nigeria has one of the largest HIV burdens globally. HIV-positive cancer patients require ART coordination.
drug_interactions: Rifampicin (TB co-treatment) — reduces dolutegravir levels, dose adjustment needed
Cisplatin + Tenofovir — additive nephrotoxicity risk, monitor creatinine closely
Doxorubicin + ART — monitor for hepatotoxicity overlap

malaria_coinfection.prevalence_context: Malaria is endemic in Nigeria. Cancer patients on chemotherapy have increased susceptibility.
interaction_risks: Fever differential diagnosis complication (malaria vs neutropenic fever)
Artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) generally safe with most chemo

controller_constraints.fever_workup: mRDT + blood culture before assuming neutropenic fever

sickle_cell_disease.prevalence_context: 2-3% of Nigerian population is HbSS. Critical comorbidity affecting drug metabolism and organ function.
drug_interactions: Hydroxyurea already causes myelosuppression — additive with chemotherapy
Chronic haemolysis affects bilirubin levels — confounds hepatotoxicity monitoring
Vaso-occlusive crises may mimic treatment side effects

controller_constraints.rationale: Dehydration triggers sickling crises; aggressive hydration during chemo

anaemia.prevalence_context: Highly prevalent in Nigeria. Significant comorbidity in cancer patients undergoing chemotherapy.

resource_aware_constraints.description: Nigeria-specific resource availability constraints for realistic treatment planning

controller_impact.avoid_protocols_requiring: rarely_available drugs unless confirmed in stock
controller_impact.rationale: Treatment interruptions due to drug unavailability are common — controller should prefer robust, available regimens

safety_thresholds_nigeria.description: Adjusted safety thresholds accounting for Nigerian patient population characteristics

hematologic.ANC_rationale: Higher threshold than CTCAE default (1000) due to higher baseline infection risk in tropical setting
hematologic.platelets_rationale: Higher threshold due to endemic malaria-related thrombocytopenia risk
hematologic.hemoglobin_rationale: Adjusted for high baseline anaemia prevalence

renal.rationale: More conservative due to dehydration risk, sickle cell nephropathy prevalence

hepatic.rationale: Adjusted for endemic hepatitis B/C and sickle cell haemolysis
